,case when min(rt.DateTime) is NULL
then 'More than a year'
else concat(substring(tostring(min(rt.DateTime)),6,2),'-',substring(tostring(min(rt.DateTime)),9,2),'-',substring(tostring(min(rt.DateTime)),1,4),' ',substring(tostring(min(rt.DateTime)),12,2),':',substring(tostring(min(rt.DateTime)),15,2))
end as [Date]
,case when min(rt.DateTime) is NULL
then ' More than a year'
else concat((hourDIFF(tolocal(min(rt.DateTime)),getdate())/24), ' Days '
,(Ceiling((HourDiff(tolocal(min(rt.DateTime)),getdate())/24.0-Floor(HourDiff(tolocal(min(rt.DateTime)),getdate())/24.0))*24)),' h '
,(Ceiling((MinuteDiff(tolocal(min(rt.DateTime)),getdate())/60.0-Floor(MinuteDiff(tolocal(min(rt.DateTime)),getdate())/60.0))*60),' m'))
end AS Duration

SELECT e.MinutesSinceKeepAlive, e.ServerName, e.IP, e.ServerType, concat(scale.PropertyValue,'x') as [Stacked Pollers], npm.PropertyValue as [NPM Polling %],e.Elements, e.Nodes, e.Interfaces, e.Volumes, sam.PropertyValue as [SAM Polling %], comp.SAMComponents, appins.AppInsightComponents, e.PollingCompletion,
CASE
WHEN PollingCompletion < 98 THEN '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Serious.png'
WHEN PollingCompletion > 98 THEN '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Check.png'
END AS [_IconFor_PollingCompletion]
, CASE
WHEN MinutesSinceKeepAlive > 2 THEN '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Serious.png'
WHEN MinutesSinceKeepAlive <2 THEN '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Check.png'
END AS [_IconFor_MinutesSinceKeepAlive]
,CASE
WHEN npm.PropertyValue> 90 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
WHEN npm.PropertyValue> 75 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Warning.gif'
ELSE '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_NPM Polling %],CASE
WHEN sam.PropertyValue> 90 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
WHEN sam.PropertyValue> 75 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Warning.gif'
ELSE '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_SAM Polling %]
,'/Orion/Admin/Details/Engines.aspx' as [_linkfor_ServerName]
FROM Orion.Engines e
left join Orion.EngineProperties npm on e.EngineID = npm.EngineID and npm.PropertyName='Orion.Standard.Polling'
left join Orion.EngineProperties sam on e.EngineID = sam.EngineID and sam.PropertyName='APM.Components.Polling'
left join Orion.EngineProperties scale on e.EngineID = scale.EngineID and scale.PropertyName='Scale Licenses'
left join (select n.engineid,count(n.Applications.Components.ComponentID) as SAMComponents
from orion.nodes n
where n.Applications.Template.Name not like 'appinsight%'
group by n.EngineID) comp on comp.engineid=e.EngineID
left join (select n.engineid,count(n.Applications.Components.ComponentID) as AppInsightComponents
from orion.nodes n
where n.Applications.Template.Name like 'appinsight%'
group by n.EngineID) appins on appins.engineid=e.EngineID

order by ServerName

##---------------Pollers & Hardware health-------------------=--------------------------###
Function Orion-UnixMemoryPollers
{
    Param(
    [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)]
    [Validatenotnullorempty()]
    $SwisConnection,
    [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)]
    [String]$NodeID,
    [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)]
    [ValidateSet('ICMP','SNMP')]
    [String]$PollingType)

      $OrionSNMPStdPollers =

         "N.Memory.SNMP.HrSwRunPerf"


    If ($PollingType -EQ 'ICMP')
    {
        $OrionDevicePollers = $OrionICMPStdPollers
    }
    ElseIf ($PollingType -EQ 'SNMP')
    {
        $OrionDevicePollers = $OrionSNMPStdPollers
    }

    ForEach ($OrionDevicePoller in $OrionDevicePollers)
    {
        $NetObjectID = $NodeID
        $NetObjectType = $OrionDevicePoller.Trim(".")[0]
        $NetObject = $NetObjectType + ":" + $NodeID
        $PollerType = $OrionDevicePoller

        $DevicePollerAdd = @{
            NetObjectID=$NetObjectID;
            NetObjectType=$NetObjectType;
            NetObject=$NetObject;
            PollerType=$PollerType;
            }

    $AddDevicePollers = New-SwisObject $SwisConnection -EntityType "Orion.Pollers" -Properties $DevicePollerAdd

    }

    Return $AddDevicePollers

}

title: PIVOT Multi UDP SAM Part 1
description: SWQL-approved PIVOT technique for transposing multi-statistic data to columns
code:
-- |--------------------|--------------------|--------------------|--------------------|--------------------|
--
-- In traditional SQL you can pivot the results (transpose columns to rows)
-- SWQL dosen't support this, so the only way to get that informaiton is to make multiple connections to the multiple element table
-- Steps:
--  1) Build the initial query getting what you need from the first entity
--     SELECT [ET].Caption
--          , [ET].IPAddress
--     FROM [Orion.EntityTable] AS [ET]
--  2) Use a LEFT JOIN to connect that entity to the multiple element entity and add an additional filter for the element you want to retrieve
--     LEFT JOIN [Orion.EntityMultiLookupTable] AS [EMLT]
--       ON [ET].ElementID = [EMLT].ElementID
--       AND [EMLT].ElementName = 'AccountStat'
--  3) And add "[EMLT].ElementValue AS [AccountStat]" to your list of properties to return
--     SELECT [ET].Caption
--          , [ET].IPAddress
--          , [EMLT].ElementValue AS [AccountStat] <-- new
--     FROM [Orion.EntityTable] AS [ET]
--     LEFT JOIN [Orion.EntityMultiLookupTable] AS [EMLT]
--       ON [ET].ElementID = [EMLT].ElementID
--       AND [EMLT].ElementName = 'AccountStat'
--  4) Since we want multiple elements from that target table, we just repeat with new aliases and element filters
--     SELECT [ET].Caption
--          , [ET].IPAddress
--          , [EMLT].ElementValue AS [AccountStat]
--          , [EMLT2].ElementValue AS [NumDrives]
--          , [EMLT3].ElementValue AS [HasDvdRom]
--     FROM [Orion.EntityTable] AS [ET]
--     LEFT JOIN [Orion.EntityMultiLookupTable] AS [EMLT]
--       ON [ET].ElementID = [EMLT].ElementID
--       AND [EMLT].ElementName = 'AccountStat'
--     LEFT JOIN [Orion.EntityMultiLookupTable] AS [EMLT2]
--       ON [ET].ElementID = [EMLT2].ElementID
--       AND [EMLT2].ElementName = 'NumDrives'
--     LEFT JOIN [Orion.EntityMultiLookupTable] AS [EMLT3]
--       ON [ET].ElementID = [EMLT3].ElementID
--       AND [EMLT3].ElementName = 'HasDvdRom'
-- The above query should return everything we want.ADDDATE
-- Note:
--   It can get confusing when you link in a single table multiple times, so coming up with good alases can help.  You'll see better aliases used below

##---------Assigning base template to the source node--------------------###
$applicationId = (Invoke-SwisVerb $swis "Orion.APM.Application" "CreateApplication" @(
    # Node ID
    $nodeId,

	# Application Template ID
    $applicationTemplateId,

	# Credential Set ID
    $credentialSetId,

	# Skip if duplicate (in lowercase)
    "false"
)).InnerText

# Check if the application was created
if ($applicationId -eq -1) {
	LogWrite "Application wasn't created. Likely the template is already assigned to node and the skipping of duplications are set to 'true'."
	exit 1
}
else {
	LogWrite "Application created with ID '$applicationId'."
}

default { "Unknown ($($swNode.Status))" }
                                     }
            }
        }
    }

    # Sort by SW Status then IP for easy reading
    $matches = $matches | Sort-Object SW_Status, IP_Address

    Write-Log "Retired in CMDB AND monitored in SolarWinds: $($matches.Count)" "SUCCESS"

    if ($matches.Count -eq 0) {
        Write-Log "No overlap found - no retired CMDB CIs currently monitored in SW."
        return
    }

    # Export
    $dateStamp  = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd_HHmmss"
    $reportFile = "$($Config.RetiredOutputDir)\Retired_Still_Monitored_$dateStamp.csv"
    $latestFile = "$($Config.RetiredOutputDir)\Retired_Still_Monitored_LATEST.csv"

    # Write timestamped file
    $matches | Export-Csv -Path $reportFile -NoTypeInformation -Force

    # Verify timestamped file exists before copying to LATEST
    if (Test-Path $reportFile) {
        # Remove old LATEST first to avoid lock issues
        if (Test-Path $latestFile) {
            Remove-Item -Path $latestFile -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        }
        Copy-Item -Path $reportFile -Destination $latestFile -Force
        if (Test-Path $latestFile) {
            Write-Log "Report saved : $reportFile ($($matches.Count) unique IPs)" "SUCCESS"
            Write-Log "LATEST saved : $latestFile" "SUCCESS"
        } else {
            Write-Log "LATEST copy failed - writing directly instead." "WARN"
            $matches | Export-Csv -Path $latestFile -NoTypeInformation -Force
            Write-Log "LATEST saved (direct write): $latestFile" "SUCCESS"
        }
    } else {
        Write-Log "Timestamped file not found after Export-Csv - writing both directly." "WARN"
        $matches | Export-Csv -Path $reportFile -NoTypeInformation -Force
        $matches | Export-Csv -Path $latestFile -NoTypeInformation -Force
        Write-Log "Both files written directly." "SUCCESS"
    }

    # Console summary
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "  ============================================" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  RETIRED IN CMDB - STILL MONITORED IN SW" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  ============================================" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "  Total unique IPs: $($matches.Count)" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    Write-Host ""
    $matches | Format-Table IP_Address, CMDB_CI_Name, SW_NodeName, SW_Status -AutoSize
    Write-Host ""

    return $reportFile
}
# =============================================================================
# MAIN
# =============================================================================
Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "SN CMDB Auto-Download START - $(Get-Date)"
Write-Log "============================================================"

Initialize-Dirs

$cookieHeader = $null
$authMethod   = "SavedCookies"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Try saved cookies first (avoids Chrome entirely if valid)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
$saved = Get-SavedCookies
if ($saved) {
    $cookieHeader = Build-CookieHeader -CookieJson $saved
    if (-not (Test-SessionValid -CookieHeader $cookieHeader)) {
        Write-Log "Saved cookies expired - will use Chrome to refresh." "WARN"
        $cookieHeader = $null
    }
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: If no valid session - use Chrome to get fresh cookies
# Chrome opens SN directly, handles Okta SSO automatically
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if (-not $cookieHeader) {
    $authMethod = "ChromeSSO"

    Start-ChromeWithCDP

    $tabId = Wait-ForSNPageLoad

    if (-not $tabId) {
        Write-Log "ServiceNow did not load in time. Exiting." "ERROR"
        Stop-Chrome
        Write-Host "Press any key to close..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
        $null = $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey('NoEcho,IncludeKeyDown')
        exit 1
    }

    $cookies = Get-ChromeCookies -TabId $tabId

    if (-not $cookies -or $cookies.Count -eq 0) {
        Write-Log "No cookies captured from Chrome. Exiting." "ERROR"
        Stop-Chrome
        Write-Host "Press any key to close..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
        $null = $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey('NoEcho,IncludeKeyDown')
        exit 1
    }

    Save-Cookies -Cookies $cookies

    $parts = foreach ($c in $cookies) { "$($c.name)=$($c.value)" }
    $cookieHeader = $parts -join "; "

    # Skip session test for fresh Chrome cookies
    # Chrome just successfully loaded SN - cookies are valid by definition
    Write-Log "Fresh Chrome cookies ready - proceeding to download." "SUCCESS"
    # NOTE: Chrome stays open - Download-CMDBReport uses it via CDP
    # Stop-Chrome is called after download completes
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: Download the report - Chrome must still be open for CDP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
$csvFile = Download-CMDBReport -CookieHeader $cookieHeader

# Now safe to minimize Chrome - download is complete
<#if ($authMethod -eq "ChromeSSO" -or $authMethod -eq "ChromeSSO-Retry") {
    Stop-Chrome
}#>

# If download failed with saved cookies, force a Chrome refresh
if (-not $csvFile -and $authMethod -eq "SavedCookies") {
    Write-Log "Download failed with saved cookies. Forcing Chrome refresh..." "WARN"
    $authMethod = "ChromeSSO-Retry"

    Start-ChromeWithCDP
    $tabId   = Wait-ForSNPageLoad

title: SN CMDB SW EXE Part 2 (Part 3/3)
description: ServiceNow CMDB integration with SolarWinds for CI reconciliation (Part 3 of 3)
code:
$cookies = Get-ChromeCookies -TabId $tabId
    Save-Cookies -Cookies $cookies

    $parts = foreach ($c in $cookies) { "$($c.name)=$($c.value)" }
    $cookieHeader = $parts -join "; "

    Stop-Chrome
    $csvFile = Download-CMDBReport -CookieHeader $cookieHeader
}

if (-not $csvFile) {
    Write-Log "Report download failed. Check log: $($Config.LogFile)" "ERROR"
    Write-Host ""
    Write-Host "FAILED - Check log: $($Config.LogFile)" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "Press any key to close..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    $null = $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey('NoEcho,IncludeKeyDown')
    exit 1
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: Validate, summarize, cleanup
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
$validation = Validate-CSV -FilePath $csvFile
Write-Summary -Validation $validation -CsvFile $csvFile -AuthMethod $authMethod
Invoke-Cleanup

Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "COMPLETE - Auth: $authMethod | Rows: $($validation.Rows)" "SUCCESS"
Write-Log "Output: $csvFile"
Write-Log "============================================================"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5: SolarWinds comparison - retired CMDB CIs still monitored
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "STEP 5 - SolarWinds Retired CI Comparison"
Write-Log "============================================================"

$swNodes = Get-SWMonitoredIPs

if ($swNodes) {
    $retiredReport = Compare-RetiredCIsWithSW -CsvFile $csvFile -SwNodes $swNodes
    if ($retiredReport) {
        Write-Log "Retired CI report: $retiredReport" "SUCCESS"
    }
} else {
    Write-Log "SolarWinds query skipped or failed - check SwisUser/SwisPass in Config." "WARN"
    Write-Log "To enable: set SwisUser and SwisPass in the Config block at top of script."
}

Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "ALL DONE" "SUCCESS"
Write-Log "============================================================"

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "All done! Output: $($Config.OutputDir)" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Retired report : $($Config.RetiredOutputDir)" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host "Log file       : $($Config.LogFile)" -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ""
Write-Host "Press any key to close..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
try { $null = $Host.UI.RawUI.ReadKey('NoEcho,IncludeKeyDown') } catch {}

title: SQL Job Fail
description: SWQL dashboard query for SolarWinds Orion
code:
SQL Job Fail:

SELECT N.Caption,D.DatabaseName,D.RecoveryModel,D.LastBackup,D.DaysFromLastBackup,D.DisplayStatus,D.TransactionLogFreeSpaceInPercents,D.DatabaseFreeSpaceInPercents
FROM APM_SqlBbDatabaseAlertsData D
Inner join NodesData N ON D.NodeID = N.NodeID
Where (DaysFromLastBackup is NULL or DaysFromLastBackup > 2) AND DatabaseName not in('tempdb','master','msdb','model')
Order by DaysFromLastBackup DESC

AD Account lock:

SELECT
              LogFile,
			  EventCode,
			  TimeGeneratedUtc,
			  ComputerName,
			  SourceName,
			  (SUBSTRING(Message,CHARINDEX('Computer Name:',Message)+15,20)) as 'Lockout Source',
              (SUBSTRING(
			         --Text to Substring
                     Message,
                     --Start
					 CHARINDEX('Account Name:',Message,CHARINDEX('Account Name:',Message)+15)+15,
					 --Length
					 CHARINDEX('Additional Information:', Message, CHARINDEX('Account Name:',Message,CHARINDEX('Account Name:',Message)+15)) - (CHARINDEX('Account Name:',Message,CHARINDEX('Account Name:',Message)+15)+15)

					 )
			  )
              as 'Lockout User',
              Message
FROM
              APM_WindowsEvent_Detail
WHERE
              EventCode = '4740' AND TimeGeneratedUtc >= DATEADD(HOUR, -24, GETDATE())
ORDER BY TimeGeneratedUtc DESC

Oracle TableSpace name:

SELECT n.Caption AS Node,a.Name AS DB,ce.AvgStatisticData AS Percentage,ce.ErrorMessage AS Tablespace
FROM Orion.APM.Component(nolock=true) c
JOIN Orion.APM.CurrentComponentStatus(nolock=true) ccs ON c.ComponentID = ccs.ComponentID
JOIN Orion.APM.ChartEvidence(nolock=true) ce ON ce.ComponentStatusID = ccs.ComponentStatusID
JOIN Orion.APM.Application(nolock=true) a ON c.ApplicationID = a.ApplicationID
JOIN Orion.Nodes(nolock=true) n ON a.NodeID = n.NodeID
WHERE c.ComponentName like 'Used Space in Tablespac%' AND ce.ErrorMessage NOT LIKE 'Query must return at least 1 row.'

code:
##--------------------------------InternalDebuglog----------------------------------------------##
$DatevalueToAppendLogName = (get-Date).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd_HH-mm-ss")
$LogFileName = "Service_Monitoring_Catlog"+$DatevalueToAppendLogName
$LogfilePath = "C:\YOUR_SCRIPTS_PATH\LogFolder\$LogFileName.log"

$tmp_LogfilePath = "C:\YOUR_SCRIPTS_PATH\LogFolder\Temp_$LogFileName.log"

New-Item $tmp_LogfilePath -type file -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

New-Item $LogfilePath -type file
Add-Content $LogfilePath "--------------------------$LogFileName ----------------------------"
#--------------------------------Credentials----------------------------------------------------##
$hostname = "YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME";
$username = "YOUR_USERNAME"
$Password = YOUR_PASSWORD
Import-Module SwisPowerShell
#--------------------------- Connect to the source system----------------------------------------###

#---------------------------Log--------------------------------------------------------------------##
function LogWrite
{
   Param ([string]$logstring)
   $logstring | Out-File $tmp_LogfilePath -Append
}

function clean_up_logfile
{
    Move-Item -Path $tmp_LogfilePath -Destination $LogfilePath
}


#---------------------------Collect the URL and server hostname--------------------#

function get-service-Monitor-tickets
{

    $server = 'YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME'
    $url = "https://${server}:8089/services/search/jobs/export"
    $splunk_usr = "YOUR_USERNAME"
    $splunk_pwd = ConvertTo-SecureString "YOUR_PASSWORD" -AsPlainText -Force
    $cred = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential($splunk_usr,$splunk_pwd)

    $search = "| inputlookup Self_URL_Monitor.csv"
    $body = @{
    search = $search
    output_mode = "csv"
    earliest_time = "-5m"
    latest_time = "-1m"
        }

add-type @"
    using System.Net;
    using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates;
    public class TrustAllCertsPolicy : ICertificatePolicy {
        public bool CheckValidationResult(
            ServicePoint srvPoint, X509Certificate certificate,
            WebRequest request, int certificateProblem) {
            return true;
        }
    }
"@
[System.Net.ServicePointManager]::CertificatePolicy = New-Object TrustAllCertsPolicy

    # This will allow for self-signed SSL certs to work
    [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12
    [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::ServerCertificateValidationCallback = { $true }
    $SearchResults = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri $url -Credential $cred -Body $body -TimeoutSec 300 | ConvertFrom-Csv
    return $SearchResults
}
$Tickets=get-service-Monitor-tickets
#$Ap_C=$Tickets.cf_title
#$Tickets=$Tickets[1]
Foreach($C in $Tickets)
{
$Ap_C=$C.cf_title
$Node=Get-SwisData $swis "SELECT top 1 [data].[Caption] AS [Caption],[data].[NodeID] AS [NodeID],[data].[Vendor] AS [Vendor],[data].[IPAddress] AS [IPAddress]
FROM orion.nodes AS data
WHERE
((([data].[CustomProperties].[Application_Code]) = ('$Ap_C')))"
$Caption=$Node.Caption
$URL=$C.URL
$Application_Name="$($Ap_C)"+"- "+ "$($C.URL)"
$nodeId = $Node.NodeID
#----------------------------Assign Base windows process application template----------------------###
if (!$nodeId) {
    ##LogWrite "Can't find node with IP '$Caption'."
    exit 1
}

# Select the URL template
$template = "URL"
$applicationTemplateId = Get-SwisData $swis "SELECT ApplicationTemplateID FROM Orion.APM.ApplicationTemplate WHERE Name=@template" @{ template = $template }

if (!$applicationTemplateId) {
    ##LogWrite "Can't find template with name '$template'."
    exit 1
}

$credentialSetID= -3

title: Stack SWQL
description: Switch stack health monitoring with member details and memory utilization
code:
SELECT TOP 10
    N.Caption,
    (M.Index / 1000) AS SwitchNumber,
    ROUND(M.AvgPercentMemoryUsed, 0) AS MemUtilPercent,
    M.TimeStampUTC AS [Time],

    -- STACK LEVEL DETAILS
    SS.MemberCount,
    SS.MasterNumber,
    SS.StatusDescription AS StackStatus,
    SS.DataRingStatusDescription AS DataRing,
    SS.PowerRingStatusDescription AS PowerRing,

    -- STACK MEMBER DETAILS
    SSM.RoleDescription,
    SSM.Model,
    SSM.SerialNumber,
    SSM.SoftwareImage,
    SSM.PowerAvailable,
    SSM.PowerUsed

FROM Orion.MemoryMultiLoadCurrent AS M
INNER JOIN Orion.Nodes AS N
        ON M.NodeID = N.NodeID

-- Safe joins — no calculated fields here
INNER JOIN Orion.NPM.SwitchStack AS SS
        ON SS.NodeID = N.NodeID

INNER JOIN Orion.NPM.SwitchStackMember AS SSM
        ON SSM.NodeID = N.NodeID
       AND SSM.SwitchNumber = (M.Index / 1000)

ORDER BY MemUtilPercent DESC

title: Syslog wig Query 1
description: Syslog message widget/dashboard configuration
code:
SELECT Count(*) AS [Total]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Emergency]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Emergency' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Emergency]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Critical.png' AS [_iconfor_Emergency]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Alert]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Alert' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Alert]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Critical.png' AS [_iconfor_Alert]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Critical]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Critical' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Critical]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Critical.png' AS [_iconfor_Critical]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Error]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Error' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Error]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Serious.png' AS [_iconfor_Error]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Warning]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Warning' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Warning]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Warning.png' AS [_iconfor_Warning]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Notice]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Notice' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Notice]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Notice.png' AS [_iconfor_Notice]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Informational]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Informational' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Informational]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/InformationalAlert.png' AS [_iconfor_Informational]

,(SELECT Count(*) AS [Debug]
FROM Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
WHERE NodeID IN ('53','977','978','979')  and let.Name = 'Syslog' AND Level = 'Debug' AND DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())) AS [Debug]
,'/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/InformationalAlert.png' AS [_iconfor_Debug]

title: Syslog wig Query 2
description: Syslog message widget/dashboard configuration
code:
--Updated Syslog Counter
SELECT
    n.Caption AS [NodeName],
    CONCAT('/ui/orionlog/logviewer/now/24hours/', ToString(n.NodeID), '/syslog') AS [_LinkFor_NodeName],
    COUNT(le.LogEntryID) AS [Total],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Emergency' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Emergency],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Critical.png' AS [_iconfor_Emergency],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Alert' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Alert],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Critical.png' AS [_iconfor_Alert],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Critical' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Critical],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Critical.png' AS [_iconfor_Critical],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Error' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Error],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Serious.png' AS [_iconfor_Error],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Warning' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Warning],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Warning.png' AS [_iconfor_Warning],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Notice' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Notice],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/Notice.png' AS [_iconfor_Notice],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Informational' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Informational],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/InformationalAlert.png' AS [_iconfor_Informational],
    SUM(CASE WHEN le.Level = 'Debug' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS [Debug],
    '/Orion/images/ActiveAlerts/InformationalAlert.png' AS [_iconfor_Debug]
FROM
    Orion.OLM.LogEntry le
JOIN
    Orion.OLM.LogEntryType let ON le.LogEntryTypeID = let.LogEntryTypeID
JOIN
    Orion.Nodes n ON le.NodeID = n.NodeID
WHERE
    le.NodeID IN ('53', '977', '978', '979')
    AND let.Name = 'Syslog'
    AND le.DATETIME >= ADDHOUR(-24, GETUTCDATE())
GROUP BY
    n.Caption, n.NodeID

title: The IP SLA are configured with the thresholds below - Priority calculated base on column value and weight. (Part 1/4)
description: Network Configuration Manager automation and config change tracking (Part 1 of 4)
code:
SELECT t1.Con, t1.[_IconFor_Con],
t1.RdpSshUrl AS [_LinkFor_Con],
t1.NodeName,
t1.[_LinkFor_NodeName],
t1.RT, t1.[_IconFor_RT], t1.Time AS MSec,
t1.PL, t1.[_IconFor_PL], t1.PacketLoss,
t1.CPU, t1.[_IconFor_CPU], t1.cLoad AS CPULoad,
t1.Memory, t1.[_IconFor_Memory], t1.rLoad AS MemoryLoad,
t1.HW, t1.[_IconFor_HW], t1.HWStat, t1.[_LinkFor_HWStat],
--
-- Priority calculated base on column value and weight.
-- wConn (Connection), scores: Down - 1000, Up - 0;
-- wTime (Response Time), scores:  > 1000ms - 300, >500ms - 200, other - 0;
-- wPacketLoss (Percent Packet Loss), scores: 100% - 1000, >30% - 300, >10% - 200, Other - 0
-- wCPU (CPU Load), scores: 100% - 150, >98% - 95, >95% - 80, Other - 0;
-- wMemory (Memory Load), scores: 100% - 150, >98% - 95, >95% - 80, Other 0;
-- wHW  (Hardware Status (worst Value)), the worst HW component status of a node with HW monitor enabled
--      scores: Down - 100, Critical - 90, Warning - 50, Up - 0, other 10
--
CASE
    WHEN  t1.wConn = 1000 THEN 1000
    ELSE  Round((t1.wTime*1.0 + t1.PacketLoss*1.0 + t1.wCPU*1.0 + t1.wMemory*1.0 + t1.wHW*1.0),1)
END AS APri
FROM
(
-------
SELECT
'ssh' AS Con, '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/small-' + ToString(a.StatusIcon) AS [_IconFor_Con],
CASE
    WHEN a.Status = 2 Then 1000  -- No Connection
    Else 0
END AS wConn,
a.NodeID, a.Vendor,
'ssh://'+ ToString(a.IPAddress) AS  RdpSshUrl,
a.Caption AS NodeName,
'/Orion/NetPerfMon/NodeDetails.aspx?NetObject=N%3a' + ToString(a.NodeID) AS [_LinkFor_NodeName],
'' AS RT,
CASE
  WHEN a.AvgResponseTime > 1000 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
  WHEN a.AvgResponseTime > 500 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up-Warn.gif'
  When a.AvgResponseTime < 0 THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Unreachable.gif'
  ELSE  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_RT],
a.AvgResponseTime AS Time,
CASE
  WHEN a.AvgResponseTime > 1000 THEN 300 -- Critical
  WHEN a.AvgResponseTime > 500 THEN  200 -- Warning
  When a.AvgResponseTime < 0 THEN  0  -- Up
  ELSE  0
END AS wTime,
'' AS PL,
CASE
  WHEN a.PercentLoss = 100 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Down.gif'
  WHEN a.PercentLoss > 30 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
  WHEN a.PercentLoss > 10 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up-Warn.gif'
  ELSE  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_PL],
a.PercentLoss AS PacketLoss,
CASE
  WHEN a.PercentLoss = 100 THEN 1000
  WHEN a.PercentLoss > 30 THEN 300
  WHEN a.PercentLoss > 10 THEN 200
  ELSE 0
END AS wPacketLoss,
'' AS CPU,
CASE
  When a.Status = 2  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Unreachable.gif'
  WHEN a.CPULoad  = 100 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Down.gif'
  When a.CPULoad > 90  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
  When a.CPULoad > 85  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up-Warn.gif'
  ELSE  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_CPU],
a.CPULoad AS cLoad,
CASE
  WHEN a.CPULoad  = 100 THEN 150 -- Down
  When a.CPULoad > 90  THEN  95 -- Critical
  When a.CPULoad > 85  THEN  80 -- Warning
  ELSE  0  -- Up
END AS wCPU,
'' AS Memory,
CASE
  When a.Status = 2  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Unreachable.gif'
  WHEN a.PercentMemoryUsed  = 100 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Down.gif'
  When a.PercentMemoryUsed > 90  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
  When a.PercentMemoryUsed > 85  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up-Warn.gif'
  ELSE  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_Memory],
a.PercentMemoryUsed AS rLoad,
CASE
  WHEN a.PercentMemoryUsed  = 100 THEN 150 --- Down
  When a.PercentMemoryUsed > 90  THEN  95 -- Critical
  When a.PercentMemoryUsed > 85  THEN  80 -- Warning
  ELSE  0 -- Up
END AS wMemory,
'' AS HW,
CASE
  When a.Status = 2  THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Unreachable.gif'
  WHEN g.HWStatusID = 5 THEN '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Critical.gif'
  When g.HWStatusID = 0 THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up.gif'
  When g.HWStatusID = 2 THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Unknown.gif'
  When g.HWStatusID = 4 THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Up-Warn.gif'
  When g.HWStatusID = 6 THEN  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Down.gif'
  ELSE  '/Orion/images/StatusIcons/Small-Unreachable.gif'
END AS [_IconFor_HW],
CASE
  When a.Status = 2  THEN 'Unreachable'
  WHEN g.HWStatusID = 5 THEN 'Critical'
  When g.HWStatusID = 0 THEN  'Good'
  When g.HWStatusID = 2 THEN  'Unknown'
  When g.HWStatusID = 4 THEN  'Warning'
  When g.HWStatusID = 6 THEN  'Down'
  ELSE  'n/a'
END AS HWStat,
'/Orion/NetPerfMon/NodeDetails.aspx?NetObject=N%3a' + ToString(a.NodeID) AS [_LinkFor_HWStat],
CASE
  WHEN g.HWStatusID = 5 THEN 90 -- Critical
  When g.HWStatusID = 4 THEN 50 --  Warning
  When g.HWStatusID = 6 THEN 100 -- Down
  When g.HWStatusID = 2 THEN 10 -- Other
  ELSE  0  -- Up
END AS wHW
FROM Orion.Nodes AS a
JOIN Orion.NodesCustomProperties AS b ON a.NodeID = b.NodeID
LEFT JOIN
(SELECT c.NodeID AS NodeID,
 MAX(CASE
  WHEN c.Status=1 THEN 0  -- UP
  WHEN c.Status=9 THEN 1  -- Unmanaged
  WHEN c.Status=0 THEN 2  -- Unknown
  WHEN c.Status=12 THEN 3  -- Unreachable
  WHEN c.Status=3 THEN 4  -- Warning
  WHEN c.Status=14 THEN 5  -- Critical
  WHEN c.Status=2 THEN 6   -- Down
  ELSE 1
  END
) AS HWStatusID
FROM Orion.HardwareHealth.HardwareCategoryStatus AS c
JOIN Orion.HardwareHealth.HardwareItem AS e ON e.HardwareCategoryStatusID = c.ID
Group BY c.NodeID
) AS g ON g.NodeID = a.NodeID

title: The IP SLA are configured with the thresholds below - Query 10
description: Network Configuration Manager automation and config change tracking
code:
Select N.Caption, N.UnManageFrom, N.UnManageUntil, AE.AccountID, N.StatusDescription
from Orion.Nodes N
LEFT OUTER JOIN (
SELECT NetObjectID, Max(AuditEventID) as [AuditEventID], Max(TimeLoggedUtc) as [TimeLoggedUtc]
FROM Orion.AuditingEvents
where ActionTypeID=
(
SELECT ActionTypeID
FROM Orion.AuditingActionTypes
where ActionType = 'Orion.NodeUnmanaged'
)
group by NetObjectID
) AS [LastUnmanageEvent] on LastUnmanageEvent.NetObjectID = N.NodeID
LEFT OUTER JOIN Orion.AuditingEvents AE on AE.AuditEventID = LastUnmanageEvent.AuditEventID
Where (N.Unmanaged = TRUE OR N.UnManageFrom > GETUTCDATE())
UNION
(
Select N.Caption, Supp.SuppressFrom, Supp.SuppressUntil, AE.AccountID, N.StatusDescription
from Orion.Nodes N
INNER JOIN Orion.AlertSuppression Supp on Supp.EntityUri = N.Uri
LEFT OUTER JOIN (
SELECT NetObjectID, Max(AuditEventID) as [AuditEventID], Max(TimeLoggedUtc) as [TimeLoggedUtc]
FROM Orion.AuditingEvents
where ActionTypeID in (
SELECT ActionTypeID
FROM Orion.AuditingActionTypes
where ActionType = 'Orion.AlertSuppressionAdded'
or ActionType = 'Orion.AlertSuppressionChanged'
)
group by NetObjectID
) AS [LastMuteEvent] on LastMuteEvent.NetObjectID = N.NodeID
LEFT OUTER JOIN Orion.AuditingEvents AE on AE.AuditEventID = LastMuteEvent.AuditEventID
WHERE (
ToUTC(Supp.SuppressFrom) >= TOLOCAL(GETDATE())
OR
ToUTC(Supp.SuppressFrom) <= TOLOCAL(GETDATE()) AND ToUTC(Supp.SuppressUntil) >= TOLOCAL(GETDATE())
)
)
ORDER BY N.Caption

title: View Export

code:
View Export:

#$v= Get-Content -path C:\Users\svc_pwv_swor_t\Desktop\L.txt
$v="387"
$hostname = "YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME"
$user = "Splunk"
$pass="Splunk@123"
$swis = Connect-Swis -Hostname $hostname -Username $user -Password $pass

foreach($view in $v){
function Export-Resource {
    param(
        [Parameter(Mandatory=$true)] [Validatenotnullorempty()] $swis,
        [Parameter(Mandatory=$true, HelpMessage = "What resourceID are we exporting?" )] [int] $resourceID,
        [Parameter(HelpMessage = "Do we need to save this resource to an XML file?" )] $exportXML,
        [Parameter(HelpMessage = "What path should the XML be saved to?" )] $exportXMLpath
    )

     $Resource = get-swisdata $swis @"
select distinct ResourceID, ViewColumn, Position, replace(replace(ResourceName,'&','ampersand'),'"','doublequotes') as ResourceName, ResourceFile, replace(replace(ResourceTitle,'&','ampersand'),'"','doublequotes') as ResourceTitle, replace(replace(ResourceSubTitle,'&','ampersand'),'"','doublequotes') as ResourceSubTitle, viewgroup
from orion.Views v
left join orion.Resources r on r.ViewID=v.ViewID
where r.resourceid = '$ResourceID'
"@


    $header = @"
<resource name="$($resource.ResourceName)" file="$($resource.ResourceFile)" column="$($resource.ViewColumn)" position="$($resource.Position)" title="$($resource.ResourceTitle)" subtitle="$($resource.ResourceSubTitle)">
<properties>
"@

    $rquery = @"
select propertyname, replace(replace(propertyvalue,char(10),'linebreak'),char(38),'ampersand') as propertyvalue
FROM resourceproperties rp
where rp.resourceid=$($resource.ResourceID)
"@
    $resourceProperties = Invoke-SwisVerb $swis 'Orion.Reporting' 'ExecuteSQL' @($rquery)

    $xmlRequest = $header
foreach ($p in $resourceproperties.childnodes.documentelement.executesqlresults) {
        $xmlRequest += '<property name = "' + $($p.propertyname) + '" value="' + ([System.Net.WebUtility]::HtmlEncode($p.propertyvalue)) + '" />'
    }
    $xmlRequest += '</properties></resource>'

    if($exportXML) {
        $namecleanup = "$($resource.ResourceTitle)_$($resource.ResourceID)"
        $namecleanup = $namecleanup.Replace("\", " ").Replace("/", " ").replace("<"," ").replace(">"," ").replace(":"," ").replace("|"," ").replace("?", " ").replace("*"," ")
        if(!$exportXMLpath) {
            $exportXMLpath ="C:\Users\svc_pwv_swor_t\Desktop\$($namecleanup).xml"
        }

        $xmlRequest | Export-Clixml -Path ($exportXMLpath + "$($namecleanup).xml")
    }
    return $xmlRequest
}


    $viewdata = (Invoke-SwisVerb $swis 'Orion.Reporting' 'ExecuteSQL' @"
select viewgroup, v.viewid, rtrim(ViewKey) AS ViewKey, rtrim(ViewTitle) as ViewTitle, case when viewgroup is null then 'NoViewGroup'
else isnull(rtrim(viewgroupname),cast(viewgroup as nvarchar)) end as viewgroupname, ViewType, ViewGroupPosition
, ViewIcon, Columns, Column1Width, Column2Width, Column3Width, Column4Width, Column5Width, Column6Width
, 'N' as System, Customizable, NOCView, NOCViewRotationInterval, vc.condition
From Views v
left join [ViewConditions]vc on vc.viewid=v.viewid
where v.viewid = $view
"@).childnodes.documentelement.executesqlresults | select-object -first 1

    $UserPath = "C:\Users\svc_pwv_swor_t\Desktop\$($viewdata.ViewGroupName)\$($viewdata.ViewTitle)\"

    " Exporting view $($viewdata.viewtitle) to $UserPath"
    if((test-path $userpath) -eq $false) {$newfolder = md -path $UserPath}
    $viewdata | Export-Clixml ($UserPath + "ViewData.xml")

    #get a list of all resources on the view we want to migrate over
    $resourceids = get-swisdata $swis @"
    select distinct ResourceID, r.ResourceTitle
    from orion.Resources r
    where viewid = $view
"@

    foreach($resourceid in $resourceids) {
        "   Exporting Resource $($Resourceid.Resourcetitle)"
        $Resource = Export-Resource $swis $resourceid.resourceid True $UserPath
    }
    }

title: EXE Build
description: ServiceNow Integration - powershell script/query
code:
# =============================================================================
# Build_EXE.ps1
# Converts SN_CMDB_Download_v4.ps1 to a standalone EXE using PS2EXE
# Run this ONCE to generate the EXE
# =============================================================================

$ScriptPath  = "C:\YOUR_PATH\Scripts\SN_CMDB_Download_v4.ps1"
$OutputEXE   = "C:\YOUR_PATH\Scripts\SN_CMDB_Download.exe"
$IconPath    = $null   # optional: "C:\YOUR_PATH\Scripts\icon.ico"

Write-Host ""
Write-Host "=======================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host " PS1 to EXE Builder" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host "=======================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ""

# Step 1: Install PS2EXE if not already installed
Write-Host "[1] Checking PS2EXE module..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
if (-not (Get-Module -ListAvailable -Name ps2exe)) {
    Write-Host "    Installing PS2EXE from PSGallery..." -ForegroundColor White
    try {
        Install-Module -Name ps2exe -Scope CurrentUser -Force -ErrorAction Stop
        Write-Host "    PS2EXE installed OK." -ForegroundColor Green
    } catch {
        Write-Host "    Install failed: $($_.Exception.Message)" -ForegroundColor Red
        Write-Host "    Try running: Install-Module ps2exe -Scope CurrentUser -Force" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        exit 1
    }
} else {
    Write-Host "    PS2EXE already installed." -ForegroundColor Green
}

# Step 2: Verify source script exists
Write-Host "[2] Checking source script..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
if (-not (Test-Path $ScriptPath)) {
    Write-Host "    Script not found: $ScriptPath" -ForegroundColor Red
    Write-Host "    Copy SN_CMDB_Download_v4.ps1 to C:\YOUR_PATH\Scripts\ first." -ForegroundColor Yellow
    exit 1
}
Write-Host "    Found: $ScriptPath" -ForegroundColor Green

# Step 3: Ensure output directory exists
$outDir = Split-Path $OutputEXE -Parent
if (-not (Test-Path $outDir)) {
    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $outDir -Force | Out-Null
}

# Step 4: Build EXE
Write-Host "[3] Building EXE..." -ForegroundColor Yellow
Write-Host "    Input : $ScriptPath" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host "    Output: $OutputEXE" -ForegroundColor White
Write-Host ""

$ps2exeParams = @{
    InputFile       = $ScriptPath
    OutputFile      = $OutputEXE
    NoConsole       = $false      # set $true for completely silent background run
    NoOutput        = $false      # set $true to suppress all output
    NoError         = $false
    RequireAdmin    = $false
    Title           = "SN CMDB Download"
    Description     = "ServiceNow CMDB Network CI Download and SW Comparison"
    Company         = "YOUR_COMPANY"
    Product         = "SN CMDB Automation"
    Version         = "4.0.0.0"
}

try {
    Import-Module ps2exe -Force
    Invoke-ps2exe @ps2exeParams -ErrorAction Stop
    Write-Host ""
    if (Test-Path $OutputEXE) {
        $size = [math]::Round((Get-Item $OutputEXE).Length / 1MB, 2)
        Write-Host "EXE built successfully!" -ForegroundColor Green
        Write-Host "Location : $OutputEXE ($size MB)" -ForegroundColor Green
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "=======================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Write-Host " NEXT STEPS" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Write-Host "=======================================" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "1. Test run:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Write-Host "   $OutputEXE" -ForegroundColor White
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "2. Schedule daily (run as admin):" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Write-Host "   See Schedule_Task.ps1 in same folder" -ForegroundColor White
        Write-Host ""
        Write-Host "3. Silent background mode:" -ForegroundColor Yellow
        Write-Host "   Re-run this script with NoConsole = `$true" -ForegroundColor White
        Write-Host ""
    } else {
        Write-Host "EXE not found after build - check for errors above." -ForegroundColor Red
    }
} catch {
    Write-Host "Build failed: $($_.Exception.Message)" -ForegroundColor Red
}

title: SN CMDB Download v4 - Compare-RetiredCIsWithSW (Part 2/2)
description: ServiceNow CMDB integration with SolarWinds for CI reconciliation (Part 2 of 2)
code:
Initialize-Dirs

$cookieHeader = $null
$authMethod   = "SavedCookies"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 1: Try saved cookies first (avoids Chrome entirely if valid)
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
$saved = Get-SavedCookies
if ($saved) {
    $cookieHeader = Build-CookieHeader -CookieJson $saved
    if (-not (Test-SessionValid -CookieHeader $cookieHeader)) {
        Write-Log "Saved cookies expired - will use Chrome to refresh." "WARN"
        $cookieHeader = $null
    }
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 2: If no valid session - use Chrome to get fresh cookies
# Chrome opens SN directly, handles Okta SSO automatically
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
if (-not $cookieHeader) {
    $authMethod = "ChromeSSO"

    Start-ChromeWithCDP

    $tabId = Wait-ForSNPageLoad

    if (-not $tabId) {
        Write-Log "ServiceNow did not load in time. Exiting." "ERROR"
        Stop-Chrome
        exit 1
    }

    $cookies = Get-ChromeCookies -TabId $tabId

    if (-not $cookies -or $cookies.Count -eq 0) {
        Write-Log "No cookies captured from Chrome. Exiting." "ERROR"
        Stop-Chrome
        exit 1
    }

    Save-Cookies -Cookies $cookies

    $parts = foreach ($c in $cookies) { "$($c.name)=$($c.value)" }
    $cookieHeader = $parts -join "; "

    # Skip session test for fresh Chrome cookies
    # Chrome just successfully loaded SN - cookies are valid by definition
    Write-Log "Fresh Chrome cookies ready - proceeding to download." "SUCCESS"
    # NOTE: Chrome stays open - Download-CMDBReport uses it via CDP
    # Stop-Chrome is called after download completes
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 3: Download the report - Chrome must still be open for CDP
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
$csvFile = Download-CMDBReport -CookieHeader $cookieHeader

# Now safe to minimize Chrome - download is complete
<#if ($authMethod -eq "ChromeSSO" -or $authMethod -eq "ChromeSSO-Retry") {
    Stop-Chrome
}#>

# If download failed with saved cookies, force a Chrome refresh
if (-not $csvFile -and $authMethod -eq "SavedCookies") {
    Write-Log "Download failed with saved cookies. Forcing Chrome refresh..." "WARN"
    $authMethod = "ChromeSSO-Retry"

    Start-ChromeWithCDP
    $tabId   = Wait-ForSNPageLoad
    $cookies = Get-ChromeCookies -TabId $tabId
    Save-Cookies -Cookies $cookies

    $parts = foreach ($c in $cookies) { "$($c.name)=$($c.value)" }
    $cookieHeader = $parts -join "; "

    Stop-Chrome
    $csvFile = Download-CMDBReport -CookieHeader $cookieHeader
}

if (-not $csvFile) {
    Write-Log "Report download failed. Check log: $($Config.LogFile)" "ERROR"
    exit 1
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 4: Validate, summarize, cleanup
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
$validation = Validate-CSV -FilePath $csvFile
Write-Summary -Validation $validation -CsvFile $csvFile -AuthMethod $authMethod
Invoke-Cleanup

Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "COMPLETE - Auth: $authMethod | Rows: $($validation.Rows)" "SUCCESS"
Write-Log "Output: $csvFile"
Write-Log "============================================================"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------
# STEP 5: SolarWinds comparison - retired CMDB CIs still monitored
# ------------------------------------------------------------------
Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "STEP 5 - SolarWinds Retired CI Comparison"
Write-Log "============================================================"

$swNodes = Get-SWMonitoredIPs

if ($swNodes) {
    $retiredReport = Compare-RetiredCIsWithSW -CsvFile $csvFile -SwNodes $swNodes
    if ($retiredReport) {
        Write-Log "Retired CI report: $retiredReport" "SUCCESS"
    }
} else {
    Write-Log "SolarWinds query skipped or failed - check SwisUser/SwisPass in Config." "WARN"
    Write-Log "To enable: set SwisUser and SwisPass in the Config block at top of script."
}

Write-Log "============================================================"
Write-Log "ALL DONE" "SUCCESS"
Write-Log "============================================================"

title: SN CMDB Download v4 - Config & Connection
description: Configuration, credentials, and connection setup for SN CMDB Download v4
code:
# =============================================================================
# SN_CMDB_Download_v4.ps1
# ServiceNow CMDB Network CI Report - Automated Downloader
#
# HOW IT WORKS:
#   1. Open Chrome (with CDP) pointing directly at the SN Report CSV URL
#   2. Okta session in Chrome profile auto-approves SSO silently
#   3. If Okta session expired - user logs in once, Chrome profile saves it
#   4. Wait for SN page to fully load (no redirect chains to track)
#   5. Extract cookies from Chrome via CDP
#   6. Use cookies in PowerShell to download the CSV report directly
#   7. Save cookies for next run (skip Chrome entirely if still valid)
#
# WHY THIS APPROACH:
#   - Chrome handles ALL redirect complexity (Okta SAML, relay state etc.)
#   - Script just waits for SN to finish loading - no URL pattern matching
#   - Chrome profile persists Okta session across runs
#   - Once cookies saved, Chrome not needed until session expires (~8-24hrs)
#
# Author : YOUR_NAME
# Server : YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME (or local PC)
# =============================================================================

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CONFIGURATION
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
$Config = @{
    # ServiceNow
    SNInstance        = "https://YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME"
    ReportId          = "YOUR_REPORT_ID"
    ReportUrl         = "https://YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME/sys_report_template.do?jvar_report_id=00c1e9e147e33ad03d9093ca216d43e4&CSV"

    # Paths
    OutputDir         = "C:\YOUR_PATH\CMDB"
    ArchiveDir        = "C:\YOUR_PATH\CMDB\Archive"
    CookieFile        = "C:\YOUR_PATH\Secure\sn_cookies.json"
    LogFile           = "C:\YOUR_PATH\Logs\cmdb_download.log"

    # Chrome
    ChromePath        = "C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe"
    ChromePort        = 9333   # Changed from 9222 - that port was already in use
    ChromeProfile     = "C:\YOUR_PATH\ChromeProfile"   # persistent profile - keeps Okta session

    # Timeouts
    PageLoadTimeoutSec = 90     # wait for SN to finish loading after SSO
    PollIntervalSec    = 3
    RequestTimeoutSec  = 60

    # Retention
    RetainDays         = 30
    MinExpectedRows    = 10

    # SolarWinds SWIS API
    SwisServer         = "YOUR_SERVER_HOSTNAME"
    SwisPort           = 17774
    SwisUser           = "YOUR_USERNAME"   # fill in your SW service account
    SwisPass           = "YOUR_PASSWORD"   # fill in password
    RetiredOutputDir   = "C:\YOUR_PATH\CMDB\Retired"
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOGGING
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: SN CMDB Download v4 - Download-CMDBReport (Part 1/2)
description: ServiceNow CMDB integration with SolarWinds for CI reconciliation (Part 1 of 2)
code:
function Download-CMDBReport {
    param([string]$CookieHeader)

    $dateStamp  = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd_HHmmss"
    $outputFile = "$($Config.OutputDir)\CMDB_Network_CIs_$dateStamp.csv"
    $latestFile = "$($Config.OutputDir)\CMDB_Network_CIs_LATEST.csv"
    $cdpBase    = "http://localhost:$($Config.ChromePort)"
    $csvUrl     = "$($Config.SNInstance)/sys_report_template.do?jvar_report_id=$($Config.ReportId)&CSV"

    # Chrome downloads the file automatically when navigated to the CSV URL
    # We trigger navigation via CDP then watch Chrome's download folder for the file
    $chromeDownloadDir = "$($Config.ChromeProfile)\Downloads"
    $defaultDownloadDir = "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads"

    # Ensure Chrome download dir exists
    if (-not (Test-Path $chromeDownloadDir)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $chromeDownloadDir -Force | Out-Null
    }

    # Set Chrome download dir via CDP before navigating
    Write-Log "Setting Chrome download path to: $chromeDownloadDir"
    try {
        $tabs  = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$cdpBase/json" -ErrorAction Stop
        $tab   = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.url -like "*service-now.com*" } | Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $tab) { $tab = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.type -eq "page" } | Select-Object -First 1 }
        $wsUrl = $tab.webSocketDebuggerUrl
        Write-Log "Using tab: $($tab.url)"

        # Use HttpClient for CDP - more reliable than raw WebSocket in PS
        $cdpHttpUrl = "$cdpBase/json/version"
        $versionInfo = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $cdpHttpUrl -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

        # Set download behavior via CDP REST (no WebSocket needed)
        $setDownload = @{
            id     = 1
            method = "Browser.setDownloadBehavior"
            params = @{
                behavior     = "allow"
                downloadPath = $chromeDownloadDir
            }
        } | ConvertTo-Json -Compress

        # Navigate Chrome to CSV URL via CDP REST activate + navigate
        $activateUrl = "$cdpBase/json/activate/$($tab.id)"
        Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $activateUrl -Method POST -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null

        # Use Page.navigate via a simple HTTP POST to CDP
        $navigateBody = "{`"id`":1,`"method`":`"Page.navigate`",`"params`":{`"url`":`"$csvUrl`"}}"
        Write-Log "Triggering Chrome download via CDP navigate..."

        # Connect WebSocket just for navigation command
        Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Net.WebSockets
        Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Threading
        $ws  = New-Object System.Net.WebSockets.ClientWebSocket
        $cts = New-Object System.Threading.CancellationTokenSource(30000)  # 30s timeout
        $ct  = $cts.Token

        $connectTask = $ws.ConnectAsync([Uri]$wsUrl, $ct)
        $connectTask.Wait(10000) | Out-Null  # 10s connect timeout

        if ($ws.State -ne [System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketState]::Open) {
            throw "WebSocket failed to connect"
        }

        $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($navigateBody)
        $seg   = New-Object System.ArraySegment[byte] -ArgumentList @(,$bytes)
        $ws.SendAsync($seg, [System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketMessageType]::Text, $true, $ct).Wait(10000) | Out-Null
        Write-Log "Navigation command sent. Waiting for Chrome to download file..."

        # Close WebSocket cleanly
        try { $ws.CloseAsync([System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketCloseStatus]::NormalClosure, "done", $ct).Wait(5000) | Out-Null } catch {}

    } catch {
        Write-Log "CDP navigation warning: $($_.Exception.Message)" "WARN"
        Write-Log "Chrome may still have triggered the download - checking download folders..." "WARN"
    }

    # Wait and watch for CSV file to appear in download folders
    $watchDirs = @(
        $chromeDownloadDir,
        $defaultDownloadDir,
        "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads",
        "$env:USERPROFILE\OneDrive - YOUR_COMPANY\Downloads",
        "$env:ONEDRIVE\Downloads",
        "$env:OneDriveCommercial\Downloads"
    ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } | Select-Object -Unique

    $downloadedFile = $null
    $waitSecs       = 120   # 2 min - file is ~2MB
    $startTime      = Get-Date

    Write-Log "Watching for downloaded CSV in: $($watchDirs -join ', ')"

    while (((Get-Date) - $startTime).TotalSeconds -lt $waitSecs) {
        foreach ($watchDir in $watchDirs) {
            if (-not (Test-Path $watchDir)) { continue }

            # Show .crdownload files so we know Chrome is actively downloading
            $inProgress = Get-ChildItem -Path $watchDir -Filter "*.crdownload" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
                          Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddSeconds(-120) }
            if ($inProgress) {
                $sizeMB = [math]::Round($inProgress[0].Length / 1MB, 2)
                Write-Host "    Download in progress: $($inProgress[0].Name) ($sizeMB MB so far)" -ForegroundColor DarkGreen
            }

            # Look for completed CSV - modified in last 3 minutes
            $recent = Get-ChildItem -Path $watchDir -Filter "*.csv" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
                      Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-3) } |
                      Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending |
                      Select-Object -First 1
            if ($recent) {
                # Wait 2s to ensure file write is complete
                Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
                # Verify size hasn't changed (file fully written)
                $size1 = $recent.Length
                Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
                $size2 = (Get-Item $recent.FullName).Length
                if ($size1 -eq $size2 -and $size2 -gt 0) {
                    $downloadedFile = $recent.FullName
                    Write-Log "Download complete: $downloadedFile ($([math]::Round($size2/1MB,2)) MB)" "SUCCESS"
                    break
                } else {
                    Write-Host "    File still writing ($size1 -> $size2 bytes)..." -ForegroundColor DarkGray
                }
            }
        }
        if ($downloadedFile) { break }
        $elapsed = [int]((Get-Date) - $startTime).TotalSeconds
        Write-Host "    Waiting for Chrome download... $([int]($waitSecs - $elapsed))s remaining" -ForegroundColor Gray
        Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
    }

    if (-not $downloadedFile) {
        Write-Log "No CSV found after ${waitSecs}s. Check: $($watchDirs -join ', ')" "ERROR"
        return $null
    }

    # Validate it's real CSV not HTML
    $firstLine = Get-Content $downloadedFile -TotalCount 1
    Write-Log "First line: $($firstLine.Substring(0,[Math]::Min(120,$firstLine.Length)))"

title: SN CMDB Download v4 - Get-ChromeCookies
description: Function Get-ChromeCookies from SN CMDB Download v4
code:
function Get-ChromeCookies {
    param([string]$TabId)
    Write-Log "Extracting cookies via CDP..."

    $cdpBase = "http://localhost:$($Config.ChromePort)"

    try {
        $tabs  = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$cdpBase/json" -ErrorAction Stop
        $tab   = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.id -eq $TabId } | Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $tab) {
            $tab = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.url -like "*service-now.com*" } | Select-Object -First 1
        }
        if (-not $tab) {
            $tab = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.type -eq "page" } | Select-Object -First 1
        }

        $wsUrl = $tab.webSocketDebuggerUrl
        Write-Log "Using tab: $($tab.url)"

        Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Net.WebSockets
        Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Threading

        $ws  = New-Object System.Net.WebSockets.ClientWebSocket
        $cts = New-Object System.Threading.CancellationTokenSource
        $ct  = $cts.Token

        $ws.ConnectAsync([Uri]$wsUrl, $ct).Wait()

        # Get ALL cookies (not just current page - gets Okta + SN cookies)
        $cmd   = '{"id":1,"method":"Network.getAllCookies","params":{}}'
        $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($cmd)
        $seg   = New-Object System.ArraySegment[byte] -ArgumentList @(,$bytes)
        $ws.SendAsync($seg, [System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketMessageType]::Text, $true, $ct).Wait()

        # Receive - use larger buffer for many cookies
        $buf    = New-Object byte[] 131072   # 128KB
        $bufSeg = New-Object System.ArraySegment[byte] -ArgumentList @(,$buf)
        $result = $ws.ReceiveAsync($bufSeg, $ct).Result
        $json   = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString($buf, 0, $result.Count)

        $ws.CloseAsync([System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketCloseStatus]::NormalClosure, "done", $ct).Wait()

        $parsed  = $json | ConvertFrom-Json
        $all     = $parsed.result.cookies

        # Capture ALL YOUR_COMPANY-related cookies
        # Must include BIGipServerpool (load balancer) + glide_user_route
        # These have different domains but are required for report download
        $snCookies = $all | Where-Object {
            $_.domain -like "*service-now.com*" -or
            $_.domain -like "*YOUR_COMPANY*"          -or
            $_.name   -like "BIGip*"            -or
            $_.name   -like "glide_*"           -or
            $_.name   -like "JSESSIONID*"       -or
            $_.name   -like "VALK_SESSION*"     -or
            $_.name   -like "UX-Token*"
        }

        Write-Log "Total cookies: $($all.Count) | Captured: $($snCookies.Count)" "SUCCESS"
        Write-Log "Cookie names: $(($snCookies.name) -join ', ')" "INFO"

        if ($snCookies.Count -eq 0) {
            Write-Log "No SN cookies found! Using all cookies as fallback..." "WARN"
            return $all
        }

        return $snCookies

    } catch {
        Write-Log "CDP cookie extraction failed: $($_.Exception.Message)" "ERROR"
        return $null
    }
}

description: Function Invoke-CDPNavigate from SN CMDB Download v4
code:
function Invoke-CDPNavigate {
    param([string]$TabId, [string]$TargetUrl)

    $cdpBase = "http://localhost:$($Config.ChromePort)"

    try {
        $tabs  = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$cdpBase/json" -ErrorAction Stop
        $tab   = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.id -eq $TabId } | Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $tab) { $tab = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.type -eq "page" } | Select-Object -First 1 }
        $wsUrl = $tab.webSocketDebuggerUrl

        Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Net.WebSockets
        Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Threading

        $ws  = New-Object System.Net.WebSockets.ClientWebSocket
        $cts = New-Object System.Threading.CancellationTokenSource
        $ct  = $cts.Token

        $ws.ConnectAsync([Uri]$wsUrl, $ct).Wait()

        # CDP Page.navigate command
        $cmd   = "{`"id`":1,`"method`":`"Page.navigate`",`"params`":{`"url`":`"$TargetUrl`"}}"
        $bytes = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($cmd)
        $seg   = New-Object System.ArraySegment[byte] -ArgumentList @(,$bytes)
        $ws.SendAsync($seg, [System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketMessageType]::Text, $true, $ct).Wait()

        # Read response
        $buf    = New-Object byte[] 4096
        $bufSeg = New-Object System.ArraySegment[byte] -ArgumentList @(,$buf)
        $ws.ReceiveAsync($bufSeg, $ct).Wait() | Out-Null

        $ws.CloseAsync([System.Net.WebSockets.WebSocketCloseStatus]::NormalClosure, "done", $ct).Wait()
        return $true

    } catch {
        Write-Log "CDP Navigate failed: $($_.Exception.Message)" "WARN"
        return $false
    }
}

function Save-Cookies {
    param($Cookies)
    $dir = Split-Path $Config.CookieFile -Parent
    if (-not (Test-Path $dir)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $dir -Force | Out-Null }

    @{
        SavedAt = (Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        SavedBy = $env:USERNAME
        Cookies = $Cookies
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5 | Set-Content -Path $Config.CookieFile -Force -Encoding UTF8

    # Note: ACL hardening skipped - requires SeSecurityPrivilege (admin only)
    # File is protected by its location in C:\YOUR_PATH\Secure\

    Write-Log "Cookies saved: $($Config.CookieFile) ($($Cookies.Count) cookies)" "SUCCESS"
}

title: SN CMDB Download v4 - Start-ChromeWithCDP
description: Function Start-ChromeWithCDP from SN CMDB Download v4
code:
function Start-ChromeWithCDP {
    Write-Log "Launching Chrome with CDP on port $($Config.ChromePort)..."

    # Record PIDs of Chrome already open by user - we will NOT kill these
    $script:PreExistingChromePIDs = @(
        (Get-Process -Name "chrome" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Id
    )
    if ($script:PreExistingChromePIDs.Count -gt 0) {
        Write-Log "Found $($script:PreExistingChromePIDs.Count) existing Chrome process(es) - leaving untouched."
    }

    # Build argument string for Chrome
    $chromeArgs = @(
        "--remote-debugging-port=$($Config.ChromePort)"
        "--user-data-dir=`"$($Config.ChromeProfile)`""
        "--no-first-run"
        "--no-default-browser-check"
        "--disable-extensions"
        "--disable-popup-blocking"
        $Config.SNInstance
    )

    # Launch Chrome using UseShellExecute=true to break job object inheritance
    # This prevents Chrome closing from sending signals back to PowerShell/ISE
    $pinfo                  = New-Object System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo
    $pinfo.FileName         = $Config.ChromePath
    $pinfo.Arguments        = $chromeArgs -join " "
    $pinfo.UseShellExecute  = $true
    $pinfo.WindowStyle      = [System.Diagnostics.ProcessWindowStyle]::Normal
    $proc                   = [System.Diagnostics.Process]::Start($pinfo)
    $script:ChromePID       = $proc.Id
    Write-Log "Chrome launched detached (PID: $($script:ChromePID)) -> $($Config.SNInstance)"
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 4
}

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# WAIT FOR SERVICENOW TO FULLY LOAD
# Simple approach: just wait until the tab URL is stable on service-now.com
# Chrome handles ALL the Okta/SAML redirect complexity internally
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

function Write-Summary {
    param($Validation, $CsvFile, $AuthMethod)
    @{
        LastRun    = (Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Status     = if ($Validation.Valid) { "SUCCESS" } else { "WARN - low row count" }
        RowCount   = $Validation.Rows
        Columns    = $Validation.Columns
        CsvFile    = $CsvFile
        LatestFile = "$($Config.OutputDir)\CMDB_Network_CIs_LATEST.csv"
        AuthMethod = $AuthMethod
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3 |
        Set-Content "$($Config.OutputDir)\cmdb_last_run.json" -Force
    Write-Log "Summary written."
}

title: SN CMDB SW EXE Part 1 - Download-CMDBReport
description: Function Download-CMDBReport from SN CMDB SW EXE Part 1
code:
function Download-CMDBReport {
    param([string]$CookieHeader)

    $dateStamp  = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd_HHmmss"
    $outputFile = "$($Config.OutputDir)\CMDB_Network_CIs_$dateStamp.csv"
    $latestFile = "$($Config.OutputDir)\CMDB_Network_CIs_LATEST.csv"
    $cdpBase    = "http://localhost:$($Config.ChromePort)"
    $csvUrl     = "$($Config.SNInstance)/sys_report_template.do?jvar_report_id=$($Config.ReportId)&CSV"

    $chromeDownloadDir  = "$($Config.ChromeProfile)\Downloads"
    if (-not (Test-Path $chromeDownloadDir)) {
        New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $chromeDownloadDir -Force | Out-Null
    }

    # Navigate Chrome to CSV URL using CDP helper
    Write-Log "Triggering Chrome download via CDP navigate..."
    try {
        $tabs = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$cdpBase/json" -ErrorAction Stop
        Write-Log "CDP tabs: $($tabs.Count)"
        $tab  = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.url -like "*service-now.com*" } | Select-Object -First 1
        if (-not $tab) { $tab = $tabs | Where-Object { $_.type -eq "page" } | Select-Object -First 1 }
        if (-not $tab) { throw "No CDP tab found" }
        Write-Log "Using tab: $($tab.url)"

        # Activate tab
        Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "$cdpBase/json/activate/$($tab.id)" -Method POST -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Out-Null

        # Navigate using CDPHelper
        $navCmd  = "{`"id`":1,`"method`":`"Page.navigate`",`"params`":{`"url`":`"$csvUrl`"}}"
        $navResp = [CDPHelper]::SendCommand($tab.webSocketDebuggerUrl, $navCmd)
        Write-Log "Navigate response: $($navResp.Substring(0,[Math]::Min(100,$navResp.Length)))"
        Write-Log "Chrome navigating to CSV - download will start shortly."

    } catch {
        Write-Log "CDP navigate warning: $($_.Exception.Message) - watching folders anyway" "WARN"
    }

    # Watch download folders
    $watchDirs = @(
        $chromeDownloadDir,
        "$env:USERPROFILE\Downloads",
        "$env:USERPROFILE\OneDrive - YOUR_COMPANY\Downloads",
        "$env:ONEDRIVE\Downloads",
        "$env:OneDriveCommercial\Downloads"
    ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) } | Select-Object -Unique

    Write-Log "Watching: $($watchDirs -join ' | ')"

    $downloadedFile = $null
    $waitSecs       = 120
    $startTime      = Get-Date

    while (((Get-Date) - $startTime).TotalSeconds -lt $waitSecs) {
        foreach ($watchDir in $watchDirs) {
            $inProgress = Get-ChildItem -Path $watchDir -Filter "*.crdownload" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
                          Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddSeconds(-120) }
            if ($inProgress) {
                $sizeMB = [math]::Round($inProgress[0].Length / 1MB, 2)
                Write-Host "    Downloading: $($inProgress[0].Name) ($sizeMB MB)" -ForegroundColor DarkGreen
            }
            $recent = Get-ChildItem -Path $watchDir -Filter "*.csv" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
                      Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -gt (Get-Date).AddMinutes(-3) } |
                      Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending |
                      Select-Object -First 1
            if ($recent) {
                Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
                $size1 = $recent.Length
                Start-Sleep -Seconds 1
                $size2 = (Get-Item $recent.FullName -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue).Length
                if ($size1 -eq $size2 -and $size2 -gt 0) {
                    $downloadedFile = $recent.FullName
                    Write-Log "Download complete: $downloadedFile ($([math]::Round($size2/1MB,2)) MB)" "SUCCESS"
                    break
                }
            }
        }
        if ($downloadedFile) { break }
        $elapsed = [int]((Get-Date) - $startTime).TotalSeconds
        Write-Host "    Waiting... $([int]($waitSecs - $elapsed))s remaining" -ForegroundColor Gray
        Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
    }

    if (-not $downloadedFile) {
        Write-Log "No CSV found after ${waitSecs}s." "ERROR"
        return $null
    }

    $firstLine = Get-Content $downloadedFile -TotalCount 1
    Write-Log "First line: $($firstLine.Substring(0,[Math]::Min(120,$firstLine.Length)))"
    if ($firstLine -like "*<html*" -or $firstLine -like "*<!DOCTYPE*") {
        Write-Log "Got HTML not CSV." "ERROR"
        return $null
    }

    Move-Item -Path $downloadedFile -Destination $outputFile -Force
    Copy-Item  -Path $outputFile    -Destination $latestFile -Force
    $size = (Get-Item $outputFile).Length
    Write-Log "Saved: $size bytes -> $outputFile" "SUCCESS"
    Write-Log "LATEST updated: $latestFile" "SUCCESS"
    return $outputFile
}

# VALIDATE CSV
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
